articles.title: State Farm Receives Approval for 6.2% Auto Rate Reduction in California
articles.snippet: The California Department of Insurance approved State Farm's 6.2% auto rate cut for new and renewing policies — less than a year after a 17.7% increase in the state.

articles.title: Why CA Auto Insurance Rates Spiked in 2026 (And How to Lower Yours)
articles.snippet: California premiums are rising about 6% in 2026 — driven by the new 30/60/15 minimums, costlier EV repairs, and wildfire-related losses. Here's how California drivers can push back.

articles.title: Average Cost of Car Insurance in California for 2026
articles.snippet: Full coverage in California now averages roughly $2,719 a year — up about 12% from 2025. Bankrate breaks down rates by city and insurer.

articles.title: California Faces Third-Highest Car Insurance Rate Hike
articles.snippet: Despite a national slowdown, California posted the third-largest auto insurance increase in the country, with Southern California drivers hit hardest.

articles.title: California's New Minimum Liability Requirements (30/60/15)
articles.snippet: Effective January 1, 2025 under SB 1107, California raised its minimums to 30/60/15 — the first change since 1967, with another increase to 50/100/25 set for 2035.

articles.title: California Low Cost Auto Insurance Program (CLCA): Who Qualifies in 2026
articles.snippet: CLCA premiums run from about $232 a year in rural counties to $887 in Los Angeles County for income-eligible good drivers — far below the state market average.

articles.title: Why Is Car Insurance So Expensive in California? (2026)
articles.snippet: Inflation in repair costs, EV claims, wildfire risk, and the higher 2025 minimums all push California rates up — even though Prop 103 keeps them regulated.

articles.title: Cheapest Car Insurance in California for 2026
articles.snippet: A comparison of the cheapest widely available insurers for California drivers, for both 30/60/15 minimum and full coverage.

articles.title: How Much Is Car Insurance for a 16-Year-Old in California?
articles.snippet: A 16-year-old in California averages around $2,500+ a year — but a family policy can cost roughly 44% less than two separate policies, before good-student discounts.

articles.title: Average Cost of Car Insurance in California for 2026
articles.snippet: Experian's look at California averages by coverage level and city, with Northern California generally cheaper than the Southern California metros.

articles.title: Best Car Insurance for New Drivers in California (2026)
articles.snippet: Which California insurers treat new drivers best, plus how the state's ban on gender as a rating factor changes the math for young men.

articles.title: Settlement Reached on State Farm's California Rate Request
articles.snippet: The Department of Insurance, Consumer Watchdog, and State Farm reached a three-party settlement in the proceeding reviewing State Farm's emergency rate request.

articles.title: California Auto Insurance Rates Jump Over 30% Since 2023
articles.snippet: A multi-year view of how California premiums have climbed since 2023, and why regional differences across the state remain large.

articles.title: Proposition 103 and California Auto Insurance
articles.snippet: The state regulator explains the prior-approval system and the mandated 20% Good Driver Discount for qualifying California drivers.

articles.title: California Low Cost Automobile Insurance Program
articles.snippet: The official CLCA program offers income-eligible good drivers affordable liability coverage that satisfies California's financial-responsibility law.

articles.title: Vehicle Insurance Requirements
articles.snippet: How California verifies insurance electronically, the proof you must carry, and what happens to your registration if coverage lapses.
